design_philosophy.core_loop: Select offensive play (4-play playbook) → anticipate defensive counter → execute ground or aerial attack → alternate possession. Simple decision tree with hidden information creates tactical tension.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Play selection prediction: offense commits to one of four plays while defense must guess the correct counter-play. Correct defensive reads collapse the line and enable sacks/interceptions.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a real NFL team (via NFLPA licensing) through a championship season using strategic play-calling. Control iconic 1987-1988 NFL rosters and compete against rivals.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Drastically simplified football (9-10 players per side vs. 11; 4-play playbook vs. full offensive/defensive schemes). Emphasizes anticipation and play selection over execution. Arcade-like abstraction prioritizes accessibility and multiplayer competitiveness over simulation depth. First console game to use real NFL player names, establishing authenticity-via-licensing as a commercial design pillar.

difficulty.continues_system: Password-based progression system in one-player mode; after each victory, player receives password to continue championship quest against next opponent.
difficulty.checkpointing: Password save system allows resume between games; no mid-game checkpoints.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game offers three modes (one-player vs. computer, two-player, coach-mode play-calling only). Computer opponent difficulty appears fixed; no explicit difficulty slider documented. Playbook limitation (4 plays) applies uniformly, making strategy depth the primary challenge vector.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Championship tournament structure in one-player mode; defeating each team advances to next opponent. 12 selectable teams (Indianapolis, Miami, Cleveland, Denver, Seattle, Los Angeles Raiders, Washington, San Francisco, Dallas, New York Giants, Chicago, Minnesota) available from start.
replayability_hooks: 12 teams with distinct rosters and play effectiveness levels
Two-player competitive mode for head-to-head play
Coach mode (play-calling only) for strategy-focused players
Different team statistics from 1987-1988 season creates asymmetric matchups
Password system enables multiple campaign saves

world_building_techniques: Team rosters and player names anchor game in real NFL context (1987-1988 season snapshot)
Home city/state identification creates geographical identity without full team branding
Real player statistics (mimicked from actual season data) reinforce sports authenticity

faction_indicators: 12 distinct NFL teams with regional/city identities (NFC: Washington, San Francisco, Dallas, New York Giants, Chicago, Minnesota; AFC: Indianapolis, Miami, Denver, Seattle, Los Angeles Raiders, Cleveland)
Real player names create parasocial investment in rosters
Team play-calling style variations (San Francisco/Miami favor passing; others mixed) establish team personalities
danger_signals: Play selection prediction mechanic can create skill-based status hierarchies in multiplayer
Computer opponent assignment in championship mode lacks documented adaptivity, potentially creating frustration breakpoints
environmental_storytelling: Team rosters reflect 1987-1988 NFL landscape, anchoring game in specific historical moment
Stat-based player differentiation (implicit from documentation) creates performance expectations aligned with real player prestige

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Tecmo Bowl NES technical documentation)
wikipedia (Tecmo Bowl - gameplay mechanics, teams, reception, and licensing details)
